summary: 4 blocking issues, 4 warnings, 6 minor. Plan is structurally sound (no one-way doors; reversible refactor; verified seams) but ships a governance gate with a tautological keystone test, an undocumented shadow-detection relaxation, an untested fail-closed path, and a guaranteed test-suite break.

blocking_issues.issue: tests/mcp/test_server.py:168 asserts set(by_name) == {11 fixed tool names} with strict equality. Tasks 8 and 12 add git_rename_feed_get and filigree_closure_gate_get but never update this test, so the full suite (Task 13) fails with certainty.
blocking_issues.resolution: Add steps to Tasks 8 and 12 to add the new tool names to the expected set in test_initialize_and_tools_list_exposes_full_agent_surface.

blocking_issues.issue: evidence.py shadow loop uses `if node is test_fn: continue`, which skips the test function's own parameter-shadow check that the current runtime gate (decorator.py:245-259) performs. A test like `def test_x(guarded):` (fixture param named after the boundary) is flagged shadowed by the runtime today but passes the new evaluator. This is an undocumented governance RELAXATION, contradicting the plan's claim that the only deliberate change is the nested-helper exercise tightening.
blocking_issues.resolution: Remove the `if node is test_fn: continue` guard (the test-fn name is never a boundary name, so the name check is harmless and the arg check must run). Add a `def test_x(guarded):` corpus case to test_evidence.py asserting code == 'shadowed'.

blocking_issues.issue: The parity test test_scan_and_runtime_gate_agree_on_a_shared_corpus calls evaluate_test_evidence twice with identical args and asserts equality — it proves determinism, not parity. It never invokes scan_policy_boundaries or check_policy_boundary, and does not exercise the differing call-site inputs ({node.name} vs {func.__name__, wrapped.__name__}; src='' fallback). The design spec's keystone verification is unfulfilled.
blocking_issues.resolution: Replace the body with a parametrized test that builds a shared on-disk corpus of @policy_boundary subjects + pinned tests, drives scan_policy_boundaries(src, repo_root=...) and check_policy_boundary(func, resolver) independently, and asserts their ok/not-ok verdicts match per case.

blocking_issues.issue: Fail-closed tamper path is untested end-to-end. Task 9 get_by_issue_id calls verify() but no test feeds a forged record to the REAL ledger. Task 10 only proves the pure function re-raises via a fake. Task 11 has no BindingError->500 API test and no ALLOW (200/allowed:true) test against a real recorded binding. For a governance RC, both the blocked-on-tamper and the allowed-on-real-evidence paths must be exercised end-to-end.
blocking_issues.resolution: Add: (a) test_get_by_issue_id_raises_on_tampered_ledger using real AuditStore+forged signature (mirror test_forged_signature_is_rejected); (b) test_closure_gate_500_on_integrity_failure (mirror test_combinations_api.py:288-300); (c) test_closure_gate_200_on_real_binding using BindingLedger.record() then the HTTP endpoint.

warnings.issue: test_closure_gate_404_when_ledger_disabled is env-fragile: create_app auto-builds a BindingLedger from LEGIS_HMAC_KEY when binding_ledger=None, so the test returns 409 (not 404) if the key is set in CI.
warnings.resolution: Add monkeypatch.delenv('LEGIS_HMAC_KEY', raising=False) to the test (mirror the MCP sibling test).

warnings.issue: BindingError is not in MCP _service_error mapping (mcp.py:313-328); it falls through to INTERNAL_ERROR. HTTP maps BindingError->500. For MCP parity (and the RC2 C3 theme) a tampered ledger via filigree_closure_gate_get should surface AUDIT_INTEGRITY_FAILURE, not INTERNAL_ERROR.
warnings.resolution: Map BindingError to AUDIT_INTEGRITY_FAILURE in _service_error, and add a negative MCP test for a tampered ledger.

warnings.issue: working_tree_renames adds an injection guard on `base` but no test exercises it, unlike every sibling method (covered by test_git_surface_command_injection_mitigation).
warnings.resolution: Add pytest.raises(GitError) cases for working_tree_renames('-o') and a metacharacter ref.

warnings.issue: Both new CLI tests monkeypatch scan_policy_boundaries, so CLI->scanner argument wiring is unverified; since src/ has no decorators, a wiring bug would let CI pass silently.
warnings.resolution: Add one non-mocked test_cli.py test: write a temp file with a weak @policy_boundary, run the CLI, assert rc == 1.

recommendations.issue: Line drift: Task 8 says dispatch insertion at line 870; actual git_rename_list dispatch is line 861. Locate by content.
Task 3 says insert before 'return 1' fall-through; actual fall-through is 'return 2' at cli.py:273.
Task 7 says 'Create tests/api/test_git_api.py' but the file already exists.
get_by_issue_id 'last binding wins' semantics has no test.
recommendations.resolution: Add a two-binding test asserting the second wins.

recommendations.issue: build_rename_feed with include_worktree=True and zero worktree renames yields status='committed_only', conflating 'checked, clean' with 'not checked'.
recommendations.resolution: Consider a distinct status or worktree_checked flag; add a test.

recommendations.issue: No contract-lock test for /git/rename-feed; the object response shape differs from /git/renames (array), which Clarion's parser expects. Risk surfaces when the deferred Clarion re-point lands.
recommendations.resolution: Add a contract-lock test for the new endpoint shape in/with the deferred Clarion task.

reality.note: B1; plus env-fragile 404 test; line drifts; one false-positive (json import) discarded.

architecture.note: No one-way doors. B2 shadow relaxation + tautological parity test.

quality.note: B4 fail-closed/ALLOW paths untested end-to-end; BindingError MCP mapping.

systems.note: Verified zero @policy_boundary in src/ and no broken rule_id consumers. CI no-op + L3-widening warnings.
